articles.title: What the jury will actually decide in the case of Elon Musk vs. Sam Altman
articles.description: Here's what the biggest tech court case of the year is all about.

articles.title: Elon Musk’s SpaceXAI has been bleeding staff since its merger
articles.description: More than 50 employees have reportedly left Elon Musk’s newly merged SpaceXAI since February, raising questions about burnout, leadership changes, talent poaching, and whether liquidity events weakened retention incentives.

articles.title: OpenAI says Codex is coming to your phone
articles.description: The update gives users enhanced flexibility over how they can manage their workflows.

articles.title: Lovable just backed a company that’s looking to bring vibe coding to hardware
articles.description: Hardware company Atech raised $800,000 in pre-seed funding, including from a16z’s scout fund, Sequoia Scout Fund, and Nordic Makers.

articles.title: Cerebras IPO makes billions for Benchmark but VC Eric Vishria almost didn’t take the meeting
articles.description: Benchmark almost never backs hardware startups. So Eric Vishria dragged his feet ten years ago before agreeing to hear Cerebras' pitch.

articles.title: What happens when AI starts building itself?
articles.description: Richard Socher's new $650 million startup wants to build an AI that can research and improve itself indefinitely — and he insists it will actually ship products.

articles.title: OpenAI is reportedly preparing legal action against Apple; it wouldn’t be the first partner to feel burned
articles.description: OpenAI is so frustrated with Apple over a ChatGPT integration that failed to deliver the subscribers and prominence it expected that the company is now actively exploring legal action against the iPhone maker.

articles.title: Clawdmeter turns your Claude Code usage stats into a tiny desktop dashboard
articles.description: A new open source gadget called Clawdmeter turns Claude Code usage stats into a tiny desktop dashboard for AI coding power users.

articles.title: YouTube viewers watch 2 billion hours of Shorts on TVs each month
articles.description: Short-form video is built for mobile, so it may seem counterintuitive, but it's true: YouTube Shorts are becoming quite popular on the big screen.

articles.title: Cerebras raises $5.5B, then stock pops $108%, in the first huge tech IPO of 2026
articles.description: A year ago, it looked like this day would never happen for Cerebras.

articles.title: OpenAI says hackers stole some data after latest code security issue
articles.description: OpenAI said the damage was limited to the employees’ devices and did not affect user data nor its production systems, and none of its intellectual property was stolen.

articles.title: Spotify to adopt Apple’s new video podcast tech, offering creators easier cross-platform distribution
articles.description: Spotify will let creators distribute and monetize video podcasts on Apple Podcasts using Apple’s HLS streaming technology, without changing their existing workflows.

articles.title: Khosla Ventures is betting $10M on Ian Crosby, whose first startup, Bench, imploded
articles.description: Crosby is building Synthetic, a fully autonomous AI bookkeeping service for other startups.

articles.title: How to turn off Instagram’s new Instants feature and retract photos you accidentally shared
articles.description: While the Meta-owned social network billed Instants as a new format to share real-life moments as they’re happening, many users are looking for a way to turn off the feature, especially those who have accidentally sent images to others, not fully understanding how the feature works.

articles.title: A spyware investigator exposed Russian government hackers trying to hijack Signal accounts
articles.description: A group of likely Russian government hackers tried to hack a security researcher who investigates spyware attacks. He was then able to turn the tables on the hackers and reveal details of their espionage campaign.

articles.title: Cisco cuts nearly 4,000 jobs to spend more on AI, reports ‘record quarterly revenue’
articles.description: This is Cisco's latest layoff in recent years, while the company's chief executive touts record revenue and growth.

articles.title: Wirestock raises $23M to supply creative multimodal data to AI labs
articles.description: The company pivoted to being a data provider in 2023 and now supplies datasets of images, videos, design assets, and gaming and 3D content to AI labs.

articles.title: Two weeks left: Startup Battlefield 200 applications close May 27
articles.description: Your shot at VC access, global visibility, TechCrunch coverage, and $100K equity-free funding is running out. Deadline to apply is May 27. Apply now.

articles.title: Uber to open 2 campuses in India to support product development, operations
articles.description: The expansion comes as Uber seeks new growth areas beyond ride-hailing and invests more heavily in AI, automation, and autonomous vehicle technologies.

articles.title: Who decides what AI tells you? Campbell Brown, once Meta’s news chief, has thoughts
articles.description: "The conversation is sort of happening in Silicon Valley around one thing, and a totally different conversation is happening among consumers," Campbell Brown said at StrictlyVC.

articles.title: Men use "vocal fry" more than women, counter to stereotype
articles.description: Study suggests "the bias is real but socially constructed, rather than grounded in how women actually sound."

articles.title: Fired hacker twins forget to end Teams recording, capture own crimes
articles.description: One little mystery—solved.

articles.title: Cell phone users can't stop incriminating themselves
articles.description: People confide almost everything to their phones.

articles.title: Energy supplier abandons Lake Tahoe residents to serve data centers
articles.description: Town’s 49,000 California residents compete with Nevada data centers for energy.

articles.title: Over a year later, AMD is bringing improved FSR 4 upscaling to its older GPUs
articles.description: FSR 4.1 running on RDNA3 or RDNA2 GPUs may take a bigger performance hit.

articles.title: Judge probes whether Musk settlement with Trump admin is tainted by corruption
articles.description: Trump admin wants to let Musk pay $1.5M fine to settle $150 million Twitter suit.

articles.title: Zero-day exploit completely defeats default Windows 11 BitLocker protections
articles.description: It's not entirely clear how the exploit works. Microsoft says it's investigating.

articles.title: Your doctor’s AI notetaker may be making things up, Ontario audit finds
articles.description: Made-up therapy referrals, incorrect prescriptions among the common mistakes.

articles.title: Vaporware or not? Aptera assembles its first five validation models.
articles.description: The three-wheel, two-seat EV has been in development since 2006.

articles.title: Cisco announces record revenue and 4,000 layoffs in the same day
articles.description: Layoffs are "not a savings-driven restructure," CFO says.

articles.title: Forecasters predict wildfires, floods, severe heatwaves from incoming El Niño
articles.description: Ocean heat plus human-caused global warming is a grim recipe for deadly climate extremes.

articles.title: Motorola Razr Fold review: Fits neatly in your pocket but not your budget
articles.description: The Razr Fold has a lot going for it, but like all foldables, it's wildly expensive.

articles.title: Desperate Trump taps "Tim Apple," Jensen Huang, Elon Musk to attend Xi summit
articles.description: Xi meeting may force Trump to pivot on chip restrictions and Taiwan.

articles.title: Solar drone with jumbo jet wingspan broke a flight record—then it crashed
articles.description: The final flight and complex legacy of a pioneering solar-powered aircraft.

articles.title: FCC angers small carriers by helping AT&T and Starlink buy EchoStar spectrum
articles.description: Approval is no surprise after FCC chair pressured EchoStar to sell licenses.

articles.title: Protein in Homo erectus teeth suggests Denisovans gave us some of their DNA
articles.description: Distinct form of tooth protein in Homo erectus shows up in Denisovans—and us.

articles.title: Foiled plot tried to sneak 49 lbs of cocaine into Australia via Xerox printers
articles.description: The drugs had an estimated worth of over $9 million USD.

articles.title: AI invades Princeton, where 30% of students cheat—but peers won't snitch
articles.description: Old "honor code" systems are under strain.

articles.title: The physics of how Olympic weightlifters exploit barbell's "whip"
articles.description: The type of bar matters when it comes to how it bends and recoils, but why is still a mystery.

articles.title: NASA provides some details about Artemis III, but hard decisions remain
articles.description: "NASA also is defining the concept of operations for the mission."

tech.summary:
今日科技动态摘要

1. **OpenAI与马斯克法律战及安全事件**：马斯克诉奥特曼案进入陪审团裁决阶段；OpenAI遭遇代码安全漏洞导致数据被盗；同时OpenAI计划对苹果采取法律行动，并宣布Codex编程工具将登陆手机端。
2. **AI行业重大资本与人事变动**：Cerebras以55亿美元估值上市首日暴涨108%，其早期投资人Benchmark及风投Eric Vishria获利丰厚；马斯克的SpaceXAI合并后员工持续流失；Khosla Ventures投资1000万美元支持前Bench创始人Ian Crosby的新项目。
3. **AI自主进化与硬件新趋势**：探讨AI开始自我构建的潜在影响；Lovable投资的初创公司试图将“氛围编程”理念引入硬件领域；开发者推出Clawdmeter工具，可将Claude代码使用统计转化为桌面仪表盘。
4. **平台内容与安全动态**：YouTube Shorts电视端月观看量达20亿小时；Spotify将采用苹果新视频播客技术以简化跨平台分发；Instagram推出Instants功能并支持撤回误发照片。
5. **网络安全与间谍活动**：间谍软件调查人员揭露俄罗斯政府黑客试图劫持Signal账户，引发对通讯安全的新担忧。

**今日最值得关注的发展**：Cerebras以55亿美元估值成功上市并暴涨108%，成为2026年首个大型科技IPO，标志着AI芯片领域在资本市场的重大突破。

Learn what happened, what was affected, and how OpenAI is strengthening defenses against evolving software supply chain threats.

ai.summary:
今日AI动态摘要

1. **开源多语言嵌入模型发布**：IBM推出Granite Embedding Multilingual R2，支持32K上下文长度，在低于1亿参数的模型中达到最佳检索质量，采用Apache 2.0开源协议。
2. **AI工具与平台扩展**：Google AI将AI驱动的Google Finance功能扩展至欧洲；OpenAI展示Codex在金融、工程、广告创意等领域的应用，并推出跨平台工作能力及Windows安全沙箱。
3. **模型训练与推理优化**：Hugging Face发布连续批处理异步化方案、AWS基础模型训练与推理构建模块，以及EMO预训练混合专家模型，推动模块化与效率提升。
4. **安全与供应链防护**：OpenAI回应TanStack npm供应链攻击，并强调在敏感对话中提升ChatGPT上下文识别能力，同时构建安全的Codex Windows运行环境。
5. **AI应用案例与趋势**：AutoScout24通过AI工作流扩展工程能力；NVIDIA工程师利用Codex开发；ChatGPT在2026年初用户群体进一步拓宽，显示AI助手普及加速。

**今日最值得关注的发展是：开源社区与商业巨头同步发力多模态、长上下文及安全可控的AI基础能力，同时AI工具正从技术圈渗透至金融、广告、汽车等垂直行业的实际业务中。**

cctv.summary:
今日新闻联播动态摘要

1. **中美关系与科技竞争**：美国批准向10家中国公司出售H200芯片，英伟达CEO寻求突破；同时，美国提出“六号交易团队”计划以挑战中国在稀土领域的主导地位，并推动中美对话以防止非国家行为体获取AI模型。

2. **国际政治与外交动态**：美国国务卿马尔科·鲁比奥虽被禁止入境中国，但出现在北京引发关注；特朗普团队中的对华鹰派人物减少，而多位CEO（包括马斯克和英伟达CEO）随特朗普访华，显示经贸关系微妙变化。

3. **中国科技与产业进展**：中国新能源汽车渗透率在4月达到历史性的61.4%，传统燃油车销量大幅下滑；中国计划以500亿美元估值投资DeepSeek，并开始规范数字人技术发展。

4. **地缘政治与安全议题**：伊朗战争对中国经济和地缘政治的影响被关注；东京审判揭露靖国神社的扭曲历史叙事；中美在月球探索竞赛中展开较量。

5. **全球治理与AI监管**：OpenAI提出建立包括中美在内的全球AI治理机构；中美就确保非国家行为体不获取先进AI模型进行谈判。

**今日最值得关注的发展**：中美在科技与稀土领域的竞争加剧，同时双方高层对话与商业合作仍在推进，显示出复杂博弈中的务实一面。
